description: Safety hazards associated with environmental compliance activities at cement plants including CEMS maintenance, stack testing, and chemical handling.

hazards.title: CEMS Calibration Gas Handling
hazards.description: CEMS calibration requires handling compressed gas cylinders containing toxic, corrosive, or asphyxiant gases including SO2, NO, CO, HCl, and high-concentration span gases.
specific_risks: Toxic gas exposure from cylinder leaks or regulator failures (SO2, NO, CO, HCl)
Asphyxiation in enclosed CEMS shelters from gas release displacing oxygen
Compressed gas cylinder falling or valve breaking causing projectile hazard
Regulator failure causing over-pressurization of sample system
required_ppe: Respiratory protection available on standby for toxic gas cylinders
controls: Ensure CEMS shelter has adequate ventilation (minimum 6 air changes per hour)
Install fixed gas detection in CEMS shelter for CO and O2 depletion
Secure all cylinders in upright position with chains or straps
Use proper regulators matched to gas type and cylinder connection
Replace cylinders before pressure drops below minimum operating level
Post SDS for all calibration gases in CEMS shelter
Never use damaged or expired calibration gas cylinders

hazards.title: Stack Testing at Height
hazards.description: Emissions stack testing requires working at elevated platforms on the stack or ductwork, typically 50-300 feet above ground, with heavy test equipment and in proximity to hot exhaust gases.
specific_risks: Falls from elevated stack test platforms
Hot gas exposure when inserting or removing test probes from stack ports
Burns from hot probe equipment and sample lines
Lifting heavy test equipment (probe assemblies, sample boxes) to elevated platforms
Exposure to weather conditions (wind, lightning, rain) at height
Structural failure of poorly maintained test platforms or access ladders
required_ppe: Full body harness with shock-absorbing lanyard or SRL
controls: Inspect all fall protection equipment before each use
Verify stack test platform structural integrity before loading equipment
Use platform weight limits and do not exceed with equipment and personnel
Install hoisting equipment for lifting heavy test gear (do not carry up ladders)
Check weather forecast and suspend testing during lightning, high winds (>25 mph), or icing
Ensure stack ports have proper caps and are depressurized before opening
Minimum 2 persons on the test platform at all times
Establish emergency rescue plan for person suspended in fall arrest

hazards.title: Environmental Chemical Handling
hazards.description: Environmental compliance activities may involve handling chemicals including activated carbon for mercury control, ammonia/urea for SNCR, dry sorbent (lime, sodium bicarbonate), and laboratory chemicals for sample analysis.
specific_risks: Ammonia exposure from SNCR system leaks (irritant, toxic at high concentration)
Activated carbon dust is combustible (dust explosion risk in enclosed spaces)
Lime dust (calcium oxide) causes severe skin and eye burns
Laboratory chemical exposure during sample preparation and analysis
Sodium bicarbonate dust inhalation from dry sorbent injection systems
required_ppe: Chemical splash goggles or safety glasses with side shields
Chemical-resistant gloves appropriate for the specific chemical
Dust mask (N95 minimum) or respirator as required by chemical
Emergency eyewash and shower within 10 seconds of chemical handling areas
controls: Maintain current SDS for all chemicals and post in use areas
Follow spill response procedures specific to each chemical
Ensure ammonia detection system is operational on SNCR system
Ground and bond all activated carbon handling equipment to prevent static ignition
Store incompatible chemicals separately per NFPA 400
Train all personnel handling chemicals in HAZCOM requirements
